creator: lioux@uol.com.br
text:
> the sntpclock program from the sysutils/clockspeed port reports time
> that is off by about 22 seconds.  This just about corresponds with the
> number of leap seconds added since the "epoch" used as the basis time.
> the leapsecs.dat file should adjust for these, but it is not
> installed.

	The missing leapsecs.dat is a mistake on my part.
I overlooked this when writing the port since I've always had it
around. Not an excuse though. :)
	Also, I choose not to install this one a long time. I do not
even remember why. I think there was some resistance on the ports folk.
	Besides, there are instructions under
/usr/local/share/doc/clockspeed/INSTALL. Though, I should advertise
this more actively when the port is installed.

> however, even with the file installed, it still gets my clock off by
> about 22 seconds from the reference ntp server...

	This is a known artifact and should be documented somewhere on
the the clockspeed site.
	A fix is as follows:

	- I'll update the port but you already have the leapsecs.dat
installed. Install leapsecs.dat under /usr/local/etc/clockspeed
	- killall clockspeed (you can't have it running)
	- sntpclock IP.OF.NTP.SERVER | clockadd
	- start clockspeed
	- sntpclock IP.OF.NTP.SERVER >
/usr/local/etc/clockspeed/adjust
	- cd /usr/src/share/zoneinfo
	- make -DLEAPSECONDS all install

	This last step will build support for leapseconds handling
within the builtin time routines. I use it at home but the FreeBSD
core users advised against my automatic build with the port. I'll
add a pkg-message to the port.

	- /stand/sysinstall -> configure -> time zone (reconfigure
time zone)
	- sntpclock IP.OF.NTP.SERVER | clockadd

	Check how off is clock after these steps.

	Try this and let me know how your system reacts.
And... thanks for reminding me of it. :) I'll try to fix this for good
this time.

	Regards,

-- 
Mario S F Ferreira - UnB - Brazil - "I guess this is a signature."
lioux at ( freebsd dot org | linf dot unb dot br )
flames to beloved devnull@someotherworldbeloworabove.org
feature, n: a documented bug | bug, n: an undocumented feature
